source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
summary: PendleConnector contracts can lose out on funds when minting LP tokens.
root_cause: Pendle deposit integration mishandles minted LP/accounting outputs, causing connector funds to be lost during market deposit
broken_invariants: the connector path must not allow the condition described by "Loss of funds in PendleConnector.depositIntoMarket()" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: PendleConnector contracts can lose out on funds when minting LP tokens.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Remove the call to market.skim() and integrate with MarketMathCore.addLiquidity() to pre-calculate the used SY and PT amounts which then can be supplied to market.mint(). This will ensure no tokens are lost.
